name: operator-local-codex-devloop
description: Operator-grade local coding workflow using Codex in ~/dev/temp with a real file-edit-test-repair loop.

playbooks.name: Local Codex Two Sum Dev Loop

operator_scenarios.name: Local Codex Two Sum Dev Loop

steps.label: prepare_codex_workspace
steps.prompt: Please prepare a Codex workspace in ~/dev/temp/con-bench-twosum by launching Codex there and preparing a paired local shell target for file and test work.

steps.label: create_code_and_tests
steps.prompt: Using that Codex target plus its paired local shell target, create a small Python two_sum implementation and a unittest-based test file, then run `python3 -m unittest -q`.

steps.label: repair_without_new_target
steps.prompt: Now break one unittest on purpose. If Codex is waiting at a trust or continue prompt for this directory, accept it in the same target. Then ask Codex to fix the failing test without creating a new target, and rerun `python3 -m unittest -q`.

steps.label: summarize_target_reuse
steps.prompt: Summarize which pane or target you used for each step.
